mnemonics.title: EXC_RETURN Bit Positions
mnemonics.content: Think: 'F H S M' - FPU(4-6), Handler/Thread(1), Stack(2), Mode(0). Bits: 6-4=FPU context, 3=always1, 2=SP(0=MSP,1=PSP), 1=Mode(0=Handler,1=Thread), 0=always1

acronyms.usage: Stores return address during subroutine calls (BL/BLX)
Value in LR that encodes how to return from exception handler
Default stack pointer used in reset and handler mode
Stack pointer used in thread mode for isolation
Coprocessor whose context saving is indicated by EXC_RETURN bits 4-6

visualTips.visualization: Draw a 32-bit register: bits 31-4 all 1, bit 3=1, bit 2=SPchoice(MSP/PSP), bit 1=Mode(Thread/Handler), bit 0=1. Add column: FPUbits at 4-6
Group by last nibble: F1/F9=NoFPU, E1/E9/E9=FPU. First digit: F=noFPU, E=withFPU. Second hex: 1=Handler, 9=Thread. Third: 9/MSP, D/ED/ED=PSP

analogies.analogy: Like a bookmark in a book - you place it at your current page (return address) before turning to a new chapter (subroutine), so you can find your place again.
Like a 'return ticket' that tells the exception handler which 'platform' (stack/mode) to return to after the 'detour' (exception) is complete.
MSP is like the main desk at a library (for staff/privileged work), PSP is like a personal study desk for individual users (thread mode).
